title: Cirrus: Add support for podman-next magic
body:
Rather than supporting a special-mode *just* for netavark/aardvark
testing [in podman CI], support testing with all the latest
`podman-next` COPR packages with a `[CI:NEXT]` magic string in ***DRAFT*** PRs.

The idea here is very similar to the netavark/aardvark special mode it
replaces.  Most podman-dependencies do not have the level of
comprehensive CI as exist here.  This new CI-mode allows testing
upstream updates to podman-dependencies without needing to roll out
a whole new package/release for them.

Also update documentation for this new mode.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: With `[CI:NEXT]` in the description, there are a bunch of [checkpoint & SELinux related system test failures](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6518642095947776).  I didn't dig into them at all, since presumably these are actual issues caused by updating to the latest podman-next COPR packages (i.e. before any strong integration & testing has happened).

> With `[CI:NEXT]` in the description, there are a bunch of [checkpoint & SELinux related system test failures](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/6518642095947776). I didn't dig into them at all, since presumably these are actual issues caused by updating to the latest podman-next COPR packages (i.e. before any strong integration & testing has happened).

err, not `caused` by podman-next, but would `need` podman-next to verify if the latest changes work on the latest changes in other repos.

> verify if the latest changes work on the latest changes in other repos.

Right, hmmmm.  Some of this use-case is already covered.  For example [this PR opened by renovate](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18510).  [But renovate isn't set to propose for everything](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/.github/renovate.json5#L65-L73).  So I can see this feature being useful to test packaging-level changes (i.e. items outside of strict code-dependencies).
@rhatdan PTAL (just to confirm), would this feature help you work on https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18439 ?
@flouthoc This affects a CI-feature you originally requested.  PTAL to confirm this will also work for your netavark/aardvark testing needs.
